scoring_rule: Unanimous-agreement: a probe matches only if all runs agree AND the agreed answer matches expected. Disputed probes (runs disagree) are not counted as matches and are reported as a probe-quality signal.

probe_results.question: Does the document mention all three of: Hydrogen, Helium, and Lithium?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source Section 1 table rows and Section 3 Glossary.

probe_results.question: In the document, are 'Hydrogen', 'Helium', and 'Lithium' presented together with their atomic numbers and atomic masses in a tabular structure (markdown table syntax with pipes, HTML <table> tags, aligned-column plain text, or any other clear table-presentation signal)?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source Section 1 has \begin{tabular}{lccc} with header row Element/Symbol/Atomic Number/Atomic Mass and three data rows. Rendered PDF shows a four-column ruled table. Probe accepts any clear table encoding.

probe_results.question: Does the document mention all four of: Moon, Mars, Jupiter, and Saturn?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source Section 2 table rows and Section 3 Glossary.

probe_results.question: In the document, are 'Moon', 'Mars', 'Jupiter', and 'Saturn' presented together with their AU distances in a tabular structure (markdown table syntax with pipes, HTML <table> tags, aligned-column plain text, or any other clear table-presentation signal)?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source Section 2 has \begin{tabular}{lc} with header row Object/Distance and four data rows. Rendered PDF shows a two-column ruled table. Probe accepts any clear table encoding.

probe_results.question: In the document, are the table column headers (such as 'Element', 'Symbol', 'Atomic Number', 'Atomic Mass', 'Object', 'Distance') visually distinguished from data cells via any clear formatting signal — for example markdown bold (**text**), HTML <strong> or <th> tags, separator-row markdown table headers, or any other encoding that marks them as headers?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source uses \textbf{} on every header cell. Rendered PDF shows bold header rows separated by \toprule/\midrule. Probe accepts any clear header-distinction signal.

probe_results.question: Does the document contain the numeric values '1.008', '4.0026', and '6.94' associated with the first three elements?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source Section 1 table data rows. Rendered PDF shows these values in the Atomic Mass column.

probe_results.question: Does the document contain a mathematical expression equivalent to 'Z = 1' (or 'Z=1') in the body text after the first table — whether as inline LaTeX ($Z = 1$), unicode math, plain-text 'Z = 1', or any other clear math-expression encoding?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source Section 1 body text contains 'the atomic number $Z = 1$'. Rendered PDF shows italicized Z, equals sign, 1. Probe accepts any clear encoding of the equation.

probe_results.question: In the document, is there a caption associated with the first (elements) table that contains the phrase 'first three elements' or equivalent wording (e.g., 'Atomic properties of the first three elements')?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source Section 1: \caption{Atomic properties of the first three elements.}. Rendered PDF shows 'Table 1: Atomic properties of the first three elements.' below or above the table.

probe_results.question: In the document, is there a caption associated with the second (distances) table that mentions 'distances' and 'solar-system objects' (or equivalent wording)?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source Section 2: \caption{Approximate distances of selected solar-system objects.}. Rendered PDF shows this caption associated with the second table.

probe_results.question: In the document, does the body text contain at least one explicit reference to a numbered table (e.g., 'Table 1', 'Table 2', or equivalent), as opposed to only describing the table's content without a label?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source uses Table~\ref{tab:elements} and Table~\ref{tab:distances} four times across body text. Rendered PDF shows 'Table 1' and 'Table 2' references resolved. Probe checks whether the table label ('Table N') survives in the converted body.

probe_results.question: Does the document mention 'Beryllium' (atomic number 4)?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source contains only Hydrogen, Helium, and Lithium (atomic numbers 1, 2, 3). Beryllium is the next element by atomic number — a thematic distractor not present in the source.

probe_results.question: In the document, is there a third table (Table 3) with its own caption?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source contains exactly two tables (Table 1: elements, Table 2: distances). Tests whether the converter or judge invents an additional table or caption.
